title: astragalus matthewsiae
intro: Astragalus matthewsiae, also known as Matthews' milkvetch, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are thought to stimulate the immune system and aid in the body's natural response to stress. The medicinal properties of Astragalus matthewsiae are believed to provide various health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune function, and reduced inflammation. Astragalus matthewsiae is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is typically consumed in doses of 6-12 grams per day to support overall health and well-being.
